trade.summary: The Stati Uniti is Corea del Sud's second-largest trading partner. Corea del Sud runs a persistent trade surplus with the Stati Uniti, which has been a source of friction. The KORStati Uniti FTA (Corea del Sud-Stati Uniti Free Trade Agreement, 2012) governs the relationship. Major Corea del Sudn exports include semiconductors (Samsung, SK Hynix), automobiles (Hyundai, Kia), ships, and electronics. Stati Uniti exports to Corea del Sud include aircraft, semiconductors (Stati Uniti-designed), agricultural products, and LNG. Corea del Sudn companies have made major investments in Stati Uniti manufacturing in response to the Inflation Reduction Act.
disputes: Stati Uniti concern over Corea del Sudn trade surplus
disputes over Corea del Sudn automobile market access

diplomatic.summary: The Stati Uniti-Corea del Sud alliance is one of the cornerstones of Indo-Pacific security. Established by the Stati Uniti-ROK Mutual Defense Treaty (1953), the alliance includes ~28,500 Stati Uniti troops stationed in Corea del Sud. The 2023 Washington Declaration reaffirmed extended deterrence commitments including nuclear umbrella assurances in response to North Corea del Sud's nuclear advances. The 2023 Camp David trilateral with Giappone represented a historic upgrade in trilateral security coordination. Corea del Sud's relationship with the Stati Uniti is its most important foreign relationship.
recent_incidents: 2023 Washington Declaration reaffirming nuclear extended deterrence
2023 Camp David trilateral with Stati Uniti and Giappone
Ongoing Stati UnitiFK (Stati Uniti Forces Corea del Sud) host nation support negotiations
Corea del Sud's Inflation Reduction Act manufacturing investment response

military.summary: The Stati Uniti-Corea del Sud military alliance is one of the world's most integrated bilateral defense relationships. The Stati Uniti stations 28,500 troops in Corea del Sud and maintains Combined Forces Command (CFC) with a Stati Uniti general as commander. The two militaries conduct extensive joint exercises (Ulchi Freedom Shield, Foal Eagle). The Stati Uniti nuclear umbrella is central to deterring North Corea del Sud. Corea del Sud is acquiring Stati Uniti-made F-35 stealth fighters, Patriot PAC-3 missiles, and other advanced Stati Uniti systems. The 2023 Washington Declaration established a new Nuclear Consultative Group.
incidents: Periodic burden-sharing negotiations over host nation support costs
US pressure on Korea to decouple from China in semiconductor supply chains

financial.summary: Deep bilateral investment ties. Corea del Sudn FDI in the Stati Uniti has surged following the Inflation Reduction Act — Samsung (Texas chip fab), LG Energy Solution, SK Battery America, and Hyundai (Georgia EV plant) have committed hundreds of billions to Stati Uniti manufacturing. Stati Uniti FDI in Corea del Sud includes major technology, financial, and consumer companies. Corea del Sudn holdings of Stati Uniti Treasuries are significant.

energy.summary: The Stati Uniti is one of Corea del Sud's largest LNG suppliers. Stati Uniti LNG exports (primarily from Gulf Coast terminals) to Corea del Sud have grown dramatically. Corea del Sud's KOGAS and private utilities hold long-term Stati Uniti LNG contracts. The energy relationship aligns with broader security and supply diversification goals.

scientific.summary: The Stati Uniti-Corea del Sud science and technology relationship is among the most productive globally. Cooperation spans semiconductor technology (Chip4 alliance), AI, space (KARI-NASA cooperation), quantum computing, biotech, and defense science. The Chip4 semiconductor alliance (Stati Uniti, Corea del Sud, Giappone, Taiwan) coordinates allied chip security. Both countries share classified defense research. Corea del Sudn students are among the largest groups in Stati Uniti graduate science programs.
